source.title: Motion Sickness
source.jurisdiction: United States federal public-health guidance for international travel; requires UAE prescribing and travel-medicine adaptation
source.population: Travelers exposed to provocative motion, with age-, pregnancy-, vestibular-, migraine-, comorbidity- and medicine-specific qualifiers.
source.setting: Pre-travel counselling and clinician assessment of symptoms consistent with motion sickness.
source.exclusions: Exact for symptom pattern, exposure, susceptibility and differential-assessment documentation; no medicine, dose, behavioural instruction or clinical action is mapped.

exact_location.heading: Signs and symptoms; Individual susceptibility and risk factors
exact_location.locator: official chapter sections “Signs and symptoms” and “Individual susceptibility and risk factors”

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording provocative motion, gradual symptom onset, nausea, vomiting, dizziness, pallor, sweating, drowsiness, headache, visual symptoms, age, pregnancy, migraine, vertigo and vestibular-history context without diagnosing automatically.

items.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.
